key_insight: A hypernetwork-conditioned hybrid physics-data surrogate can accurately predict multiscale mechanics across different microstructures, enabling efficient optimization of graded mycelium composites.
engineering_relevance: This makes FE�b2-style multiscale design of sustainable, functionally graded materials tractable and helps reduce peak stress through microstructure-aware optimization.
method: Condition a microscale surrogate on microstructural variables using a hypernetwork.
Combine physics-based structure with data-driven prediction for multiscale mechanical behavior.
Extend conditioning to manufacturing variables to link process, microstructure, and macroscopic response.
evidence_signal: The conditioned surrogate was validated against full FE�b2 simulation and used to optimize a graded disk, reducing peak stress by 42% versus a random microstructure.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the representativeness of the small training set and the fidelity of the microstructure/manufacturing variables used for conditioning.
paper_title: Microstructure-Conditioned Surrogate Models for Graded Multiscale Optimization of Mycelium Composites
